mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate indicates easing discount rates and improving liquidity conditions, which should lift equity multiples and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when the easing trend is persistent rather than a one-off move.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX forward returns are not positive in the majority of periods with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation surge forces tighter policy expectations and higher real discount rates, which should pressure valuation multiples and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when inflation is accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-surprise episodes defined by rising CPIAUCSL produce positive 20d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the conditional Rank IC is not negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in UNRATE signals deteriorating household income prospects and higher recession odds, which should reduce cyclical equity demand and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX returns are not negative on average after UNRATE crosses the 0.3pp/3m threshold, or if PAYEMS does not weaken alongside the trigger and the signal still appears predictive.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS together with rising PAYEMS indicates broad-based nominal demand and labor income growth, which should support sales expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal does not outperform either RSAFS or PAYEMS alone in walk-forward tests, or if positive 60d SPX returns are not concentrated in periods where both series are rising.

mechanism_narrative: A broad improvement in INDPRO after an earlier contraction signals supply normalization and margin relief, which should support equities with a lag and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change turns positive after at least one month of decline
failure_condition: Reject if positive reversals in INDPRO are not followed by above-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the signal loses predictive power once revision-adjusted INDPRO is used.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST below recent levels signals weakening housing demand and tighter future consumption, which should weigh on cyclical sectors and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 5% versus its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if sharp HOUST declines are followed by positive 20d SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the trigger has no measurable conditional effect versus unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS is still rising while UNRATE is flat or falling, labor income growth should sustain consumption and earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-growth signal underperforms a simple PAYEMS-only rule, or if positive 20d returns do not dominate when PAYEMS rises and UNRATE does not rise.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, the combination of rising FEDFUNDS and accelerating CPIAUCSL implies policy is becoming more restrictive relative to inflation, which should compress equity multiples and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with concurrent increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL do not show negative 20d SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the joint effect disappears once either series is included alone.
